prompt: Create the @medicine-wheel/ontology-core TypeScript package — the foundational ontology layer for the Medicine Wheel Developer Suite.

secondary.target: RDF-aligned vocabulary for Medicine Wheel concepts
TypeScript types and Zod schemas from scattered implicit data model
semantic query helpers for relational traversal, Wilson alignment, OCAP compliance
npm package publishable and consumable by both server and client
mcp-medicine-wheel and mcp-medicine-wheel-ui to consume ontology-core

assumptions: Existing types in both repos represent the implicit ontology to formalize
OCAP® flags and Wilson alignment are sufficiently defined in existing code to extract
Package should work in both Node.js (server) and browser (Next.js) environments

east.text: Vision: A single source of truth ontology package unifying the Medicine Wheel data model across server and client
Vision: RDF-aligned vocabulary that bridges Indigenous relational ontology with semantic web standards

south.text: Analysis: Audit existing types.ts in both repos to identify all entities, relationships, and constants
Analysis: Map existing implicit relationships to first-class relational entities
Analysis: Study how OCAP® flags and Wilson alignment are currently computed

west.text: Validation: Zod schemas enforce data integrity at runtime
Validation: Package compiles and is importable from both ESM server and Next.js client
Validation: Existing constants match exactly between old and new sources

north.text: Action: Create package directory structure, package.json, tsconfig.json
Action: Implement types, schemas, constants, vocabulary, and query helpers
Action: Build and verify package compiles

actionStack.text: Create package scaffold (package.json, tsconfig.json, directory structure)
Extract and formalize TypeScript type definitions from existing repos
Design relational-first schema with first-class Relation entities
Create Zod validation schemas for all types
Consolidate constants (DIRECTIONS, COLORS, ICONS) into single exports
Implement semantic query helpers (traversal, Wilson alignment, OCAP compliance)

ambiguities.text: RDF-aligned vocabulary depth unclear — full Turtle/RDFS definitions or just IRI string constants?
ambiguities.suggestion: Start with IRI string constants and TypeScript types; formal OWL/RDFS can be added later

ambiguities.text: Wilson alignment metric calculation not fully specified in existing code
ambiguities.suggestion: Define the metric interface now; actual computation can be refined iteratively

ambiguities.text: OCAP compliance check specifics not detailed
ambiguities.suggestion: Model OCAP as flags (ownership, control, access, possession) with boolean/enum checks; full policy engine is future work
